perScenario.notes: The retrieved dashboard, Next.js, shadcn/ui, browser-application, Cloudflare, preview, and production-verification guides directly support the conditional stack choice, client/server boundary, representative dense-screen check, live provider compatibility checks, approval gate, and evidence receipt. Auth, CSV, audit, and regional details were correctly identified as engineering judgment rather than attributed to Possible.

unsupportedClaims: The claim that a one-piece CNC-machined 6061-T6 bracket is already justified for this five-part lot exceeds the retrieved process-selection guidance; the transcript itself identifies load and allowable-squareness facts that remain unknown.

The exact provisional envelope, slot dimensions, datum-relative tolerances, hole pattern, fastener arrangement, and torque-retention details are not supported by the retrieved guides or their recorded sources. The guides support defining and inspecting such controls, not these numerical values.
perScenario.notes: All nine reads were relevant. The corpus supports requirements-first bracket definition, process comparison, functional tolerance and datum contracts, drawings, inspection, and revisioned handoff packages. The response appropriately labels many values provisional, but that does not make the specific values source-supported.

Presentations supports audience-led sequencing, one job per slide, notes and sources, format selection, and target-environment export checks. Art supports editable sources, licensing, and provenance, while Digital photo frame supports treating offline and privacy language as evidence-bound claims. Exact accessibility values and the claim ledger were not counted because the transcript identifies them as brief-derived editorial controls rather than Possible-derived proof.

The six reads were relevant. The local guides and recorded Three.js and React Three Fiber sources support a conservative WebGL baseline, React-led R3F selection with direct Three.js as an alternative, explicit browser/device/fallback decisions, representative-scene testing, runtime asset/input/visual/frame checks, and lifecycle verification. Exact accessibility and performance thresholds were properly disclosed as project-specific recommendations and were not counted as cited claims.

Digital photo frame directly supports the candidate device/application/storage/enclosure architecture, explicit local media storage rather than cache, offline transitions, privacy boundaries, and the distinction between local storage and encryption. Custom manufactured parts and Production web verification support controlled manufacturing packages and evidence-based web readiness. Styling, Singapore compliance, waitlist privacy, and marketing claims were correctly left for later specialist verification.

All six reads were relevant. Their text and recorded MIT, ros2_control, NIST, ISO, and OSHA sources support requirements-first design, load- and duty-based actuator sizing, explicit control interfaces and faults, simulation limits, separation of part inspection from robot performance, measurement-driven calibration, application risk assessment, and claims restricted to the tested hardware envelope. The response did not treat the guides as certification.
Focused searches returned no relevant guide, no guide was read, and the response abstained instead of force-fitting unrelated material.
Focused searches returned no relevant guide, no guide was read, and the response preserved legal and factual uncertainty instead of adapting adjacent guidance.
